articles.title: Windows Update will soon automatically roll back faulty drivers
articles.description:
Microsoft is on a mission to fix Windows 11, and part of that involves improving the often-frustrating Windows Update experience. While you'll soon be able to pause updates indefinitely, Microsoft is also adding a new feature that automatically rolls back problematic drivers that have been installed through Windows Update.
Microsoft has created a new "Cloud-Initiated Driver Recovery" feature that can replace a faulty driver installed on a PC with a previously working driver through Windows Update. Right now, Windows 11 users have to manually roll back a driver or hardware vendors have to publish a new one to work around any problems, but th …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Chronic traumatic encephalopathy symptom checklist misses the mark in 75% of cases, autopsies show
articles.description: Most people who met proposed clinical criteria meant to identify chronic traumatic encephalopathy (CTE) during life did not show hallmark brain changes of the disease at autopsy. The findings raise concerns that widespread clinical adoption of these criteria could lead to misdiagnosis and unintended mental health consequences for people at higher risk, including athletes and military veterans.

articles.title: Scientists discover hidden math secret inside Chinese money plant leaves
articles.description: Scientists have uncovered a hidden mathematical secret inside the leaves of the Chinese money plant: a naturally occurring geometric pattern known as a Voronoi diagram, something typically associated with city planning, computer science, and network design. By mapping tiny pores and looping veins in the plant’s leaves, researchers discovered that the plant organizes itself using the same kind of elegant spatial logic humans use to solve complex distance problems — without ever “measuring” anything.

articles.title: Scientists discover the strange way CO2 cools part of Earth’s atmosphere
articles.description: Scientists have finally cracked the mystery behind one of climate change’s strangest fingerprints: while Earth’s surface heats up, the upper atmosphere is rapidly cooling. Researchers at Columbia University discovered that carbon dioxide acts very differently high above the planet, where it actually helps radiate heat into space instead of trapping it. The team found that certain infrared wavelengths fall into a “Goldilocks zone” that becomes increasingly effective as CO2 levels rise, accelerating cooling in the stratosphere.

articles.title: Deadly “red sky” solar storm from 800 years ago discovered in ancient trees
articles.description: Researchers in Japan traced a hidden medieval solar storm using ancient tree rings and centuries-old sky observations. The team linked reports of eerie red auroras with spikes of carbon-14 trapped in buried wood, revealing a powerful solar radiation event around 1200 CE. The findings suggest the Sun was far more active at the time, with unusually short solar cycles.

articles.title: Who are the Japanese? Huge DNA discovery rewrites history
articles.description: Scientists analyzing the genomes of thousands of people across Japan discovered evidence for a previously overlooked third ancestral group, challenging the long-accepted “dual origins” theory. The newly identified ancestry appears linked to the ancient Emishi people of northeastern Japan. Researchers also uncovered inherited Neanderthal and Denisovan DNA connected to conditions like diabetes, heart disease, and cancer.

articles.description: Superstar actor Rajinikanth recalled a time when he stayed at the Art of Living centre near Bengaluru where to his shock, nobody recognised him. Speaking about the incident to an audience with Sri Sri Ravishankar by his side, Rajinikanth had the audience in splits when he said the incident “crushed his ego”.

dailyNews.description: Round-trip train tickets brought down to $98 from $150, and bus fares to cost $20 instead of $80, state officials say.

dailyNews.description: US sanctions imposed on UN expert Francesca Albanese by the Trump administration temporarily ⁠blocked by a judge.

dailyNews.title: Appeals court to hear arguments over Trump's orders that sought to punish law firms
dailyNews.description: An appeals court is set to hear arguments Thursday over the Trump administration's bid to reinstate executive orders that sought sanctions against four elite law firms.

dailyNews.title: Video: Mamata Banerjee in lawyer’s gown faces sloganeering at Calcutta high court during post-poll violence case hearing
dailyNews.description: Former Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee appeared as counsel at the Calcutta High Court, presenting evidence of post-poll violence. She alleged that children and women were not spared and submitted a list of alleged murder victims, urging the court to protect the people of Bengal. The Trinamool Congress lauded her commitment to truth and justice.

dailyNews.title: US Efforts to End Iran War Stumble as Ship Seized Near UAE
dailyNews.description: US efforts to end the war with Iran were dealt a setback after a commercial vessel was apparently seized by unauthorized personnel near the United Arab Emirates, increasing uncertainty over control of the critical Strait of Hormuz.

dailyNews.title: Inside Jack Schlossberg’s Chaotic Campaign to Revive Camelot
dailyNews.description: Erratic behavior and staff turnover have colored Mr. Schlossberg’s bid for a House seat in New York, raising questions about his readiness for office.

dailyNews.title: National Recording Registry adds Taylor Swift, Beyoncé, Ray Charles, more
dailyNews.description: The Library of Congress revealed this year's list of 25 recordings to be preserved for future generations on the National Recording Registry.

dailyNews.title: Workers are getting paid to teach AI how to do their jobs
dailyNews.description: AI companies are recruiting a wide range of temp workers, from writers to wine enthusiasts, for hourly-paid gigs to help train their language models.

dailyNews.title: Trump demands Medicaid data for deportation. Some states go further.
dailyNews.description: Several states have required their health agencies to take on another job: verifying immigration status among Medicaid recipients and reporting them to the U.S. Department of Homeland Security.

dailyNews.title: Senators introduce ban on lawmakers becoming lobbyists
dailyNews.description: New bipartisan legislation, first seen by CNBC, would prevent U.S. lawmakers from lobbying after they leave office.

dailyNews.title: The MAHA movement is coming to school cafeterias. Here's what that means for kids
dailyNews.description: U.S. school districts worry it could get even more expensive to prepare a meal under new federal dietary guidelines, as they also contend with cuts to programs that helped them buy local food.

dailyNews.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping had stern words for President Trump on Taiwan as they met in Beijing, warning of potential "clashes and even conflicts" if the issue isn't "handled properly."

dailyNews.title: Iran insists it's ready for a new U.S. attack as Trump in China for state visit
dailyNews.description: Iran's military says it's trained and ready for any new U.S. assault as President Trump predicted a "long talk" about the war with China's President Xi in Beijing.

dailyNews.title: Pomp and pageantry: Key moments from day one of Trump's visit to China
dailyNews.description: China welcomed US President Donald Trump with cheering children and a troop parade on Thursday, before a nearly two-hour long meeting with Xi-Jinping.

dailyNews.title: China warns Taiwan tensions could jeopardize U.S. relationship
dailyNews.description: President Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping visited the Temple of Heaven after their two-hour bilateral meeting. NBC News’ Garrett Haake reports that, according to Chinese state media, Taiwan was among the most important topics discussed.

dailyNews.title: Trump courts Xi amid Iran war and trade tensions
dailyNews.description: Trump praised China’s leader and emphasized their personal bond as both sides sought to steady relations despite tensions over Iran, trade and global power.

dailyNews.title: Why Xi Doesn’t Need a Deal With Trump
dailyNews.description: President Xi Jinping of China is buying time for Beijing and may see an opening with a U.S. president weakened by the war in Iran.

dailyNews.title: The China Gambit: From Nixon to Trump
dailyNews.description: President Trump’s visit to Beijing comes as relations with China have become more combative.

dailyNews.title: As Trump and Xi Meet in China, American and Chinese People Are Drifting Apart
dailyNews.description: Past meetings between the presidents of the U.S. and China have involved friendly displays of cultural exchange. Those gestures have largely dried up.

dailyNews.title: Iran War Powers Vote Fails Again in Senate, but GOP Opposition Grows
dailyNews.description: Senator Lisa Murkowski of Alaska became the latest Republican to switch her vote to halt the conflict and require President Trump to win congressional approval to continue it.

dailyNews.title: Trump’s Remark on Americans’ Finances and Iran War Points to GOP Midterm Challenges
dailyNews.description: Asked if he was motivated by Americans’ financial woes to make a deal to end the war with Iran, he responded, “Not even a little bit.”

dailyNews.title: Trump Administration Will Withhold $1.3 Billion in Medicaid Payments to California
dailyNews.description: Vice President JD Vance said the state had not done enough to fight fraud in the public health insurance program.

dailyNews.title: Crypto Industry Pushes a Bill to Tilt Regulation in Its Favor
dailyNews.description: After a series of political victories under President Trump, firms are lobbying Congress for a sweeping framework they helped shape.

dailyNews.title: Trump pledges to raise detained pastor's case with Xi Jinping during Beijing visit as family pleads for help
dailyNews.description: Pastor Ezra Jin's daughter speaks about her father's detention in China and Trump's pledge to raise his case with Xi Jinping during Beijing visit.
